World.headline: France arrests hundreds of rioters nationwide as PSG win Champions League
World.detail: French Ministry of Interior says 416 people detained nationwide, including 283 in Paris, after PSG's win over Arsenal. Violent clashes erupted as fans celebrated PSG's Champions League victory.

World.headline: Israel issues more displacement orders in Lebanon, seizes strategic castle
World.detail: Israeli forces advanced across Lebanon's Litani River as Lebanese and Israeli military delegations met in the US. Defence minister announces seizure of fortress as advance against Hezbollah moves beyond Litani River.
Ukraine keeps up assault on Russian oil infrastructure as Kyiv braces for more strikes. The Russian drone struck an apartment building in Romania, wounding two people, Romanian officials said.
World.headline: Ukrainian drone attacks hit multiple Russian targets, including refinery
Japan rejects 'new militarism', says China is rapidly arming
World.detail: Japan accuses China of lacking military transparency and stresses the importance of dialogue for regional stability. Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a 'peace-loving' nation while China expands military capabilities 'without sufficient transparency'.

World.headline: Dormitory fire at Kenyan girls' school kills at least 16 students
World.detail: A dormitory fire at Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Kenya has resulted in the death of at least 16 students. The incident is under investigation, and further details are awaited.

Technology.headline: After the AI binge, companies balk at soaring bills
Technology.detail: As AI adoption increases, companies are starting to reconsider their AI spending due to unexpectedly high costs, with some observing that token costs can surpass employee expenses within months of use. Some companies are even switching to free open-source AI models to cut costs.

Technology.headline: Samsung Electronics surpasses Micron Technology as world's largest automotive memory supplier
Technology.detail: Samsung Electronics has overtaken Micron Technology to become the largest supplier in the automotive memory semiconductor market, driven by the increasing demand for high-capacity memory in advanced driver-assistance systems and sophisticated infotainment systems. This growth is fueled by cutting-edge memory products like LPDDR dynamic RAM and UFS.

Technology.headline: AI-powered shopping now driving more sales than traditional searches
Technology.detail: AI-powered shopping experiences, where customers interact with conversational chatbots to make purchases, are now surpassing traditional search methods in driving sales, as consumers trust AI to provide accurate results and product recommendations.  Adobe reports a 393% growth in AI-driven traffic to retail sites in the first quarter of this year.

Technology.headline: Tech stars from the 90s reborn: Nokia, Dell and Cisco surge on AI
Technology.detail: Several tech companies from the 1990s, including Nokia, Dell, and Cisco, are experiencing a resurgence due to the expansion of AI infrastructure. Cisco, for example, has reinvented itself and is focusing on AI, with robust revenue forecasts driven by AI-related demand.

Technology.headline: Uber, Starbucks, OpenAI, TikTok And 20 Great AI Tools
Technology.detail: Uber is reevaluating its AI investment strategy due to a lack of measurable improvements from increased AI usage, and Starbucks has discontinued its AI inventory system after only nine months because it was deemed unreliable. TikTok is emerging as a viable platform for product sales, with 70% of users indicating they've purchased products based on recommendations on the platform.

Entertainment.detail: Emily Blunt clarified that she did not use AI in Steven Spielberg's Disclosure Day, expressing her fear of the technology in the entertainment industry. This comes amid ongoing discussions about the use of AI in film and other creative fields.
Entertainment.headline: Emily Blunt says she didn't use AI in Steven Spielberg's Disclosure Day: 'I'm terrified of it'
Oscar-winning 'Star Wars' editor Marcia Lucas dies at 80
Entertainment.detail: Marcia Lucas, known for her work as the editor on the original Star Wars trilogy, has passed away at the age of 80. She won an Oscar for her editing on 'Star Wars: A New Hope'.

Kanye West performed to a massive crowd of 118,000 people in Turkey, despite facing bans in other locations. This event highlights the controversial artist's continued ability to draw large audiences despite ongoing controversies.
Entertainment.headline: Kanye West performs to 118,000 in Turkey amid bans elsewhere

Entertainment.detail: Hong Kong authorities are moving to tighten regulations on claw machines due to rising addiction concerns. This comes after a court ruling in 2022 allowed the unrestricted operation of these machines, leading to a surge in public complaints.
Entertainment.headline: Hong Kong targets claw machine addiction
Celebrity Birthdays Today, May 31, 2026
Entertainment.detail: Today, May 31, 2026, marks the birthdays of several notable celebrities, including Clint Eastwood, who turns 96, Colin Farrell, who is 50, and Brooke Shields, who celebrates her 61st birthday. Also celebrating birthdays are Normani Kordei Hamilton (30), Lea Thompson (65), Darryl "D.M.C." McDaniels (62), Waka Flocka Flame (40), and Azealia Banks (35).

Zimbabwe.detail: The push to extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa's term through Constitutional Amendment No. 3 must be rejected by all Zimbabweans who value democracy, constitutionalism and accountability, as the Constitution of Zimbabwe is designed to protect citizens from abuse of power and to ensure orderly leadership renewal through regular elections. Attempts to alter constitutional term limits or extend political authority outside the spirit of democratic competition mirror the very tendencies that many Zimbabweans condemned under Mugabe's rule.
Zimbabwe.headline: Reject the dangerous push for term extension
Govt Moves To End The Use Of Mercury In Small-Scale Gold Mining
Zimbabwe.detail: The government is taking steps to end the use of mercury in small-scale gold mining.

Zimbabwe.headline: Vimbai Chikwenhere takes over as new Auditor-General
Zimbabwe.detail: Vimbai Chikwenhere has been appointed as the new Auditor-General.
A new petroleum deal is expected to benefit Zimbabwe's economy.
Zimbabwe.headline: NEW PETROLEUM DEAL A BOON FOR ECONOMY
Court order sees over 300 illegal structures demolished in Whitecliff
Zimbabwe.detail: Over 300 illegal structures have been demolished in Whitecliff following a court order.

Education.detail: A recent government review has found that the current education system is not adequately preparing children for the responsibilities and challenges of adulthood.
Education.headline: Education is failing to prepare children for adulthood, finds government review
Pressure mounts for Education Department to release research funds
Education.detail: Lawmakers are increasing pressure on the U.S. Department of Education to release nearly $300 million in unspent research funds, despite the department's claims that it will meet its statutory obligations.

Education.headline: Artificial Intelligence Teachers Say Lack of AI Guidance Is a Major Problem
Education.detail: Most teachers report they have not received formal guidance on how to effectively use AI tools in their educational work, highlighting a significant gap in support and training.

Education.headline: Reading scores hit decades-low levels, new national education report says
Education.detail: A new national education report reveals that reading scores have declined to decades-low levels, with student achievement declines beginning years before COVID-19.

Education.headline: Baldwin Wallace University winds down 16 degree programs, 19 minors
Education.detail: Baldwin Wallace University, a private nonprofit in Ohio, is discontinuing 16 degree programs and 19 minors to invest savings in areas with high student and workforce demand.

Science.headline: Tiny Microbes in Fish May Shape Ocean Chemistry
Science.detail: A new discovery suggests that tiny microbes living inside fish may be helping shape the chemistry of the world's oceans by working alongside their hosts to produce calcium.

Science.headline: Pigeons' Navigation System Found: Iron-Filled Immune Cells
Science.detail: Scientists have uncovered a surprising navigation system in pigeons: iron-filled immune cells in the liver that may act like tiny magnetic sensors, with birds deprived of these cells struggling to find their way home under overcast skies.

Losing weight may involve rewiring the gut and the brain at the same time, as shown in a study of obese adults where an intermittent fasting-style diet led to significant weight loss, healthier metabolic markers, and notable shifts in gut bacteria and brain scans.
Science.headline: Weight Loss May Involve Rewiring Gut and Brain

Science.detail: A new study suggests fish oil may help reduce insulin resistance even in people who aren't obese; in diabetic rats, omega-3 supplementation improved blood sugar levels, cholesterol, and inflammation by shifting immune cells into a more beneficial state.
Science.headline: Fish Oil May Reduce Insulin Resistance

Science.detail: A new pancreatic cancer pill may be a game changer for patients.
Science.headline: New Pancreatic Cancer Pill May Be a Game Changer for Patients

Finance & Economy.detail: Conservative leader Pierre Poilievre is calling for an emergency debate on the possibility of a recession, according to a letter addressed to Bank of Canada Governor Mark Carney.
Finance & Economy.headline: Poilievre calls for emergency debate on recession in letter to Carney
Germany 10Y Bond Yield Hits 7-week Low
Finance & Economy.detail: The Germany 10 Year Government Bond Yield decreased to 2.93%, the lowest since April 2026, and over the past 4 weeks, it lost 9.92 basis points.
A gold miner is preparing to revive a specific type of listing in Hong Kong that hasn't been witnessed in 12 years, potentially signaling renewed interest in the region's mining sector.
Finance & Economy.headline: A gold miner is set to reboot a Hong Kong listing type not seen in 12 years

Finance & Economy.detail: Labour leadership hopeful Wes Streeting says NI reduction for firms could 'incentivise' hiring, particularly of younger people.
Finance & Economy.headline: Wes Streeting calls for national insurance cut and North Sea drilling

Finance & Economy.detail: The increasing demand for AI-related technology and components is driving a surge in cargo theft, which ultimately leads to higher costs for consumers.
Finance & Economy.headline: AI demand — and tactics — drive surge in cargo theft, costing consumers more

Lifestyle.headline: After my mum died, I couldn't face tackling the clothes she left behind. But wearing them has helped me celebrate the woman she was
Lifestyle.detail: A woman shares how wearing her deceased mother's clothes has helped her cope with grief and celebrate her mother's life, describing it as a draining yet affirming process that has brought her closer to confronting life without her mother while celebrating who she was. The act of sorting and wearing the clothes has become a seismic element of her grieving process, helping her to remember and connect with her mother.

Lifestyle.headline: US adult cigarette smoking rate hits another all-time low
Lifestyle.detail: The rate of cigarette smoking among adults in the U.S. has reached a new all-time low, indicating a continued decline in tobacco use. This reflects ongoing efforts in public health to reduce smoking rates through various prevention and cessation programs.

A leading specialist reveals the daily routines, exercise mistakes and surprising lifestyle errors secretly accelerating joint wear and tear over 50.  The expert provides insights into how to avoid these common pitfalls and maintain healthy joints as you age.
Lifestyle.headline: Rheumatologist warns over-50s: 5 everyday habits fast-tracking your joint pain

Lifestyle.detail: A man writes to an advice column about his new wife who prioritizes her adult sons, one of whom is struggling with alcoholism and relies on his parents' support. The advice columnist suggests viewing the sons' issues separately and suggests a thoughtful gift for the wife, like flowers or candies that she particularly enjoys.
Lifestyle.headline: New wife won't cut apron strings for her sons
Exercise in the morning may help to boost fat metabolism, study suggests
Lifestyle.detail: A recent study suggests that exercising in the morning could be more effective in boosting fat metabolism compared to exercising at other times of the day. This finding highlights the potential benefits of incorporating morning workouts into one's daily routine for improved metabolic health.

Health.headline: Scientists Discover Hidden Gut-Brain Circuit That Triggers Protein Cravings
Health.detail: When the body runs low on protein, the gut sends powerful signals to the brain that reshape cravings and push animals to seek essential amino acids instead of sugar, according to researchers.

Health.headline: How aging reshapes sensorimotor learning: Older adults may lose explicit strategy but gain implicit adaptation
Health.detail: New research explores how aging affects sensorimotor learning, finding that older adults may lose explicit strategies but gain implicit adaptation.
A new study suggests melatonin supplements may help night shift workers boost their body's DNA repair processes, potentially offsetting some of the damage linked to working overnight.
Health.headline: Repairing DNA Damage: Scientists Discover a Surprising New Benefit of Melatonin

Health.detail: Research reveals how tau protein in Alzheimer's neurons may trigger a genetic cascade leading to cell death.
Health.headline: Inside Alzheimer's neurons, tau may set off a genetic chain reaction that ends in cell death
Specially trained service dogs helped ease PTSD symptoms in U.S. military veterans in a small study that the researchers hope will help expand options for service members.
Health.detail: A small study showed that specially trained service dogs helped ease PTSD symptoms in U.S. military veterans, and researchers hope this will expand options for service members.

Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial criticizing a Maine official for allegedly attempting to influence a vote concerning protections for girls' sports.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Leftist Maine official Shenna Bellows tries to rig vote on protections for girls' sports
GOP's $100 million Cornyn mistake backfires in Texas
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Kelly Sadler discusses the ramifications of a misstep by the GOP in Texas.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Trump's economy stronger than the headlines suggest
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Gene Marks argues that the US economy under Trump is more robust than commonly portrayed in the news.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: High school graduates should learn to embrace the unexpected
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Daniel N. Hoffman encourages high school graduates to be open to unforeseen opportunities and challenges.
An editorial expressing the growing dissatisfaction of New York residents with Mayor Zohran Mamdani.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: New Yorkers finally fed up with Mamdani

Sports.headline: Wembanyama, Spurs win the West, topple Thunder 111-103 in Game 7 to head to NBA Finals
Sports.detail: Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 7 of the Western Conference finals, advancing to the NBA Finals. Wembanyama was named West finals MVP.
Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal in a penalty shootout to win the Champions League title for the second year in a row.
Sports.headline: PSG edges Arsenal on penalties to retain Champions League title
Spurs beat reigning champions Thunder in Game 7 to set up finals date with Knicks
Sports.detail: The San Antonio Spurs defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 7 to advance to the NBA Finals where they will face the New York Knicks.

Sports.headline: Rangers look to Nolan Ryan's bloody lip moment for jersey giveaway
Sports.detail: The Texas Rangers are planning a jersey giveaway that commemorates Nolan Ryan's famous bloody lip incident.

Sports.headline: Ruthless Wigan romp to dominant win over Hull KR in Challenge Cup final
Sports.detail: Wigan Warriors secured a dominant victory over Hull Kingston Rovers in the Challenge Cup final.

Politics.headline: Former Snowbird pilots call on Ottawa to scale back aerobatic show instead of suspending it
Politics.detail: Former Snowbird pilots are asking the Canadian government to keep the iconic aerobatic show in the air by scaling back the performance rather than suspending it entirely.
A charter reform proposal could weaken the role of the Los Angeles city attorney.
Politics.headline: L.A. city attorney's role could be weakened under charter reform proposal

Politics.detail: After artists backed out of a concert series, Donald Trump proposed a 'wild' rally instead.
Politics.headline: Trump proposes 'wild' rally instead of concerts after artists back out of series
'Labour have lost their way': voters in Makerfield say it's time for a change
Politics.detail: Voters in Makerfield express that Labour has lost its way and it's time for a change.
Despite backlash, Capitol rioters are seeking payouts from Donald Trump's new 'anti-weaponization' fund.
Politics.headline: Capitol rioters clamor for payouts from Trump's new 'anti-weaponization' fund despite backlash

Crime & Justice.headline: Transgender Jurupa Valley senior AB Hernandez wins state track medals amid muted protest
Crime & Justice.detail: AB Hernandez, a transgender senior from Jurupa Valley, won state track medals amidst muted protest, highlighting ongoing debates surrounding transgender athletes' participation in sports.

Crime & Justice.headline: Family calls for justice in dog attack, killing of man on Hollywood Walk of Fame
Crime & Justice.detail: A family is demanding justice after a man was killed in a dog attack on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, raising concerns about public safety and animal control.

Federal officials allege that a worker with an anti-violence group in Los Angeles was an active member of the 18th Street gang, raising questions about the vetting processes and potential conflicts of interest.
Crime & Justice.headline: Worker with L.A. anti-violence group was active member of 18th Street gang, feds allege
Italy seizes $232m in Mafia assets tied to Cosa Nostra.
Crime & Justice.detail: A probe has uncovered luxury resorts and offshore wealth as government crackdown targets Mafia networks.
Investigators are looking for suspects after a 27-year-old man was fatally shot near his home; a dark-colored vehicle was seen pulling up before the suspects approached on foot.
Crime & Justice.headline: Toronto police seek suspects after 27-year-old fatally shot 'steps from home'
